title: Package Boundaries, Interfaces, Value Ownership, and Lifecycle · Runtime Contract: Value Ownership
summary: Callbacks mutate shared maps, packages import concrete clients and global loggers, byte slices outlive response bodies, and shutdown cannot account for goroutines. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go value ownership increment in directed owned Go crawler graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Specify ownership and copy rules for URLs, headers, byte slices, DOM selections, records, errors, and report rows crossing boundaries.
Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, audit, store, report, and command dependencies inward through consumer-owned interfaces.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Value Ownership model · Theory Value Ownership, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Go values are always copied deeply and cannot share mutable state.
